course.title: JavaScript Basics
course.description: Learn the fundamentals of JavaScript, the programming language of the web.

chapter.title: Arrays and Objects
chapter.description: Learn how to store multiple values in a single variable using arrays and objects.

lessons.title: Introduction to Arrays
lessons.explanation: An array is a special variable, which can hold more than one value at a time. You can access an array element by referring to the index number (starting at 0).

exercises.statement: Create an array called `fruits` containing 'Apple', 'Banana', and 'Cherry'. Then print the second element (Banana) to the console.
exercises.starter_code:
// Create your array below


// Print the second element
exercises.hint: Remember that array indexes start at 0, so the second element is at index 1.

lessons.title: Introduction to Objects
lessons.explanation: Objects are variables too. But objects can contain many values. The values are written as name:value pairs (name and value separated by a colon).
lessons.syntax:
let person = { firstName: 'John', lastName: 'Doe', age: 50 };
console.log(person.firstName); // Prints 'John'

exercises.statement: Create an object called `car` with properties `make` ('Toyota') and `model` ('Corolla'). Print the `make` property to the console.
exercises.starter_code:
// Create your object below


// Print the make property
